reasoning: While the document is very short (28 words), making programmatic ratios less reliable, the low shingling recall (33%) and the visual fidelity score (0.5) strongly corroborate the loss of the document's primary content: the map. Since this is a map status report, the failure to render the map, north arrow, and scale bar constitutes a functional failure that requires human intervention to determine if the visual data can be recovered.

concerns.analysis: The source document is a map-centric status report where the visual map is the primary content, positioned prominently between the title and the legend. In the HTML output, the visual organization is fundamentally altered. The legend items are converted into a bulleted list and placed above the sources, while the map itself (which is the core information) is relegated to the bottom as a broken image icon. This reversal of flow and the loss of the primary graphical element constitutes a major org
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document presents a large map of North Carolina counties as the central feature, with the legend and logos positioned around it to provide context.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output presents the legend as a list of text ('Processing for 2025 Q1', '2024 Q4', etc.) followed by the sources, with the map represented only by a broken image placeholder at the very end of the content.

concerns.analysis: The most critical visual elements of the document are either broken or missing in the HTML. The map of North Carolina, which contains the actual data of the report, is shown as a broken image icon. The NCGICC logo is also a broken image. Furthermore, the globe logo, the North arrow, and the scale bar (all meaningful spatial and branding information) are missing entirely from the rendering.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The PDF contains a colored map of NC counties, a north arrow, a scale bar, and two logos in the bottom right corner (NCGICC and a globe graphic).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML displays broken image icons with alt text: 'A map of North Carolina counties colored according to the status of their parcel updates' and 'NCGICC logo'. The globe logo, north arrow, and scale bar have no corresponding elements.

action_items: Check if the map image was captured during extraction and why it is not rendering in the HTML.
Restore the visual hierarchy so the map appears prominently before the legend and metadata.
Ensure logos and spatial indicators (North arrow, scale bar) are present and have appropriate alt-text.
